frameworks.name: individual differences in functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Brain state as a moderator of within- vs between-subject FC variability.

frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Rest treated as default but possibly suboptimal baseline for individual differences.

frameworks.name: task-evoked functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Task states may amplify trait-relevant idiosyncrasies in targeted networks.

core_question: Can brain state be manipulated to enhance individual differences in functional connectivity for biomarker discovery?
key_assumptions: Within- to between-subject variability ratio indexes individual-differences signal quality
Functional organization varies meaningfully between individuals
Rest is a neutral but not necessarily optimal baseline
manipulations: Brain state (rest vs various task conditions)
Network of interest tied to trait or behavior under study
key_findings: Preliminary HCP analyses suggest within- vs between-subject variability differs across conditions
Some task states appear to improve the individual-difference signal over rest
interpretations: State manipulation can sharpen FC-based biomarker discovery beyond resting baseline
Optimal state depends on the targeted trait and network
limitations_noted: Assumption that rest is optimal is largely untested
Preliminary analyses; open questions remain for future study
